<!-- iteration 33 | SOL xhigh | mode: model-benchmark | angle: A3-mech model-benchmark :: mechanisms & reference impls | 2026-07-15T10:51:51.935Z -->

The mode should become a task-conditional evidence and routing system, not a private leaderboard. Its strongest moat is combining private workflow items, deterministic execution receipts, calibrated judgments, real costs, and longitudinal drift into a defensible model-selection policy.

### Concrete improvements

1. **Replace aggregate sample rows with versioned trial evidence.** Emit immutable JSONL events such as `trial_dispatched`, `trial_scored`, `contamination_assessed`, `oracle_labeled`, `judge_calibrated`, `ranking_fitted`, and `selection_policy_emitted`. Each trial needs:

```text
snapshot_id, item_id, family_id, task_cluster, perturbation_id,
model/executor/prompt_hash/seed,
judge_id/version/prompt_hash/order/calibration_id,
D1..D5, deterministic receipts,
cost_usd, latency_ms, tokens,
contamination matches, canary visibility
```

DataTrove provides a useful implementation pattern: its [`NGramsDecontIndexer` and `NGramsDecontFilter`](https://github.com/huggingface/datatrove/blob/main/src/datatrove/pipeline/decont/n_grams.py) create per-task hash indexes and retain the matched n-gram and task in excluded-document metadata. Transfer that provenance behavior into benchmark items; do not reduce contamination to a global boolean.

2. **Split the item bank into confirmatory anchors and an adaptive diagnostic tail.** Calibrate deterministic binary outcomes with 2PL IRT—difficulty and discrimination—using the [`fluid_benchmarking()` entry point and `irt/fit_irt_model.py`](https://github.com/allenai/fluid-benchmarking). Quarantine negative-discrimination items, demote saturated items to smoke tests, and retain coverage quotas by task/failure family.

Adaptive item selection must not silently give competing models different confirmatory tests. Keep common sealed anchors for paired promotion evidence; use Fisher-information selection only after anchor and family-coverage obligations are met. Otherwise efficiency improves while causal comparability degrades.

3. **Upgrade the current flat bootstrap to nested, confirmatory inference.** The current [`sweep-reporter.cjs`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/lib/sweep-reporter.cjs:243) correctly pairs models by `(fixture, sample)`, but then treats those conditions as one flat resampling population. That misses task-family clustering and applies no multiplicity control.

Use:

- McNemar or paired Bayesian inference for binary pass/fail.
- A cluster bootstrap over item families, then seeds/perturbations within family, for continuous dimensions.
- Holm correction for promotion decisions across contenders and critical dimensions; BH is acceptable for exploratory diagnosis.
- Frozen confirmatory anchors for fixed-sample inference. Adaptive-tail evidence remains exploratory unless analyzed with a predeclared sequentially valid boundary.
- Non-inferiority gates on critical dimensions before utility aggregation.

[`evalstats`](https://github.com/ianarawjo/evalstats) is the closest reference implementation: paired input comparisons, nested input/run bootstrap, multiplicity correction, rank probabilities, mixed-effects support, and prediction-powered correction using sparse human labels.

4. **Turn judge calibration into a per-dimension measurement model.** Different-family judging is useful but insufficient. One April 2026 preprint found style bias much larger than position bias for its tested judges, and found mitigation effectiveness model-dependent; blanket position swapping can therefore double cost without addressing the dominant error ([paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23178), [code](https://github.com/sksoumik/llm-as-judge)).

Maintain calibration strata by `{judge version, prompt hash, task cluster, dimension}`. Use controlled invariance pairs for order, formatting/style, length, and model-family origin. Feed a randomized sparse oracle subset into prediction-powered inference, producing corrected scores and intervals rather than merely reporting judge-human agreement. Recalibration expires whenever the judge, rubric, candidate-family mix, or task distribution changes.

5. **Separate ranking from business selection.** Use Elo only as a streaming display statistic; it is order-sensitive. Fit batch Bradley–Terry for descriptive pairwise rank and Davidson/hierarchical extensions when ties, presentation order, repeated judges, or task clusters matter. [`Evalica`](https://github.com/dustalov/evalica) supplies active Rust/Python implementations of Elo, Bradley–Terry, bootstrap uncertainty, and Krippendorff’s alpha. The [`bpcs` reference implementation](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9374650/) shows the stronger model form: ties, order effects, predictors, and repeated-measure random effects.

Report `P(best)`, pairwise win probabilities, rank intervals, and cluster-specific reversals. Do not publish an authoritative single Elo number when the evidence says “task-dependent.”

6. **Wire real cost into the decision layer.** [`dispatch-model.cjs`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/dispatch-model.cjs:190) already extracts tokens and cost, but [`sweep-benchmark.cjs`](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-deep-loop/deep-improvement/scripts/model-benchmark/sweep-benchmark.cjs:460) currently writes those fields as `null`. Propagate the normalized envelope first.

Avoid `score / cost`: it becomes unstable near zero and can reward models below hard quality floors. Produce:

```text
U = Σ dimension_weight[d] × calibrated_score[d]
    − value_per_dollar × cost
    − value_per_ms × latency
    − expected_failure_cost
```

Bootstrap paired utility deltas, retain a quality/cost/latency Pareto front, and run sensitivity scenarios over prices, traffic mix, and failure costs. Unknown cost remains explicitly unrankable on the cost axis.

### Unique-value / moat thesis

Public leaderboards estimate broad average preference or capability. Manual bake-offs provide local examples but usually lose provenance, uncertainty, and repeatability. Other deep modes can research, review, or improve a candidate; they cannot establish which model/executor/prompt combination causes better outcomes on the organization’s private task distribution.

The uniqueness-sharpening output should therefore be `selection_policy.json`, not `leaderboard.json`:

```text
task_cluster → preferred model + fallback
evidence snapshot and expiry
quality/non-inferiority guarantees
cost and latency scenario
known failure families
canary and contamination status
confidence and rank instability
```

That policy can route cheap models on easy strata, reserve stronger models for high-cost failure clusters, detect when the private benchmark stops predicting production, and explain every selection from immutable trial receipts. That is unavailable to LMArena or HELM because they lack the private tasks, workflow side effects, local failure economics, and longitudinal canary lineage.

One caveat: packet `056-*` is absent from this checkout. The tie here uses the preserved benchmark-validity doctrine in packets 054/060: non-fabricated gold, deterministic Mode-A as self-consistency rather than capability, live Mode-B as corroboration, and claims constrained to what the instrument actually measures.

Open threads flagged: Simulate a crossed model-by-path-by-seed-by-perturbation design and compare fixed-sample, adaptive Rasch, and sequential confidence-bound allocators on cost, false promotion, family coverage, and causal-blame accuracy. | Specify the sealed canary lifecycle as JSONL events covering creation, proposer visibility, oracle visibility, evaluation, failure disclosure, retirement, replacement lineage, and resume. | Prototype per-dimension prediction-powered judge calibration and measure how oracle-label allocation changes when calibration is shared globally versus stratified by task cluster and candidate family. | Measure benchmark-snapshot half-life: detect when production task-cluster drift makes the private suite cease predicting live failures, then price the value of refreshing each stratum. | Test privacy-preserving portability: determine which hashed task signatures, failure taxonomies, and rank statistics can be shared across organizations without exposing private prompts while retaining model-selection value. | Elicit uncertain failure-cost and latency-value curves, then test whether the selected Pareto point remains stable under price, traffic-mix, and business-value perturbations.

Map every finding to at least one concrete system-deep-loop target (primary should be the mode "model-benchmark"): model-benchmark, runtime/convergence, runtime/fan-out-fan-in, runtime/dedup-novelty, runtime/gauges-observability, runtime/state-jsonl-checkpointing, runtime/budget-cost, runtime/locks-recovery, runtime/continuity-threading.

```json
{
  "new_repos": [{"name":"owner/repo","url":"https://...","stars":"~N or unknown","what":"one line","lesson":"transferable mechanism for this mode","maps_to":["mode-or-subsystem"],"confidence":"high|med|low"}],
  "insights": [{"insight":"one specific transferable mechanism","evidence":"repo/paper/url","maps_to":["mode-or-subsystem"],"confidence":"high|med|low"}],
  "recommendations": [{"rec":"specific actionable change","target":"mode/subsystem","rationale":"why","uniqueness":"how this sharpens the mode's unique value (or empty)","effort":"S|M|L","impact":"low|med|high","evidence":"url/repo"}],
  "contradictions": [{"claim":"","counter":"","evidence":""}],
  "next_angles": ["a specific divergent angle worth exploring next for this mode"],
  "notes": "coverage gaps you noticed"
}
```
Target 2-5 NEW repos, 2-5 insights, and 1-3 concrete recommendations (at least one uniqueness-sharpening). Novelty and verifiable specifics over volume. If the angle is exhausted, return few/no new_repos but propose sharp next_angles + recommendations (that is the divergent pivot, not a failure).

SKILL ROUTING: the repository advisor is unavailable (retryable daemon failure), so this stays a general read-only research pass. The most promising seam is not another harness: it is a paired-evidence layer combining item-level uncertainty, contamination lineage, calibrated judge error, and utility-per-cost ranking. I’m verifying four candidate implementations against that seam.

codex
One genuinely new implementation is unusually on-target: `evalstats` already exposes nested item/run bootstrap, paired tests, multiplicity correction, mixed effects, and prediction-powered correction against sparse human labels. That can become the inference spine. I’m now checking whether the newest judge-debiasing repo is reproducible enough to supply the calibration protocol, and whether contamination can be attached as item provenance rather than a one-time filter.

The mechanism stack is now coherent: Fluid Benchmarking supplies adaptive item information, DataTrove supplies match-level contamination provenance, `evalstats` supplies paired uncertainty and sparse-oracle correction, the 2026 judge study supplies controlled bias probes, and Evalica supplies fast pairwise ranking. I’m checking the local packet-056 validity conclusions before turning this into mode contracts; the main risk is treating any one of these layers as sufficient by itself.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/002-gpt-reliability-research/research/findings-registry.md:33:| F-025 | A6 | 033 benchmark methodology | CONFOUND FOUND IN OUR OWN BENCHMARK: the vague-ask scenario prompts embed fixture PATHS whose tokens (deep-loops, council-target, improvement-target) leak intent evidence to the scorer — natural-language routing is weaker than 033 measured; future scenario authoring should keep vague prompts path-free | Note for 033 package maintenance: path-free vague variants (optimize, S) | Benchmark validity improvement | S | 006 |
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/001-gpt-behavioral-hardening-research/research/lineages/sonnet-critical/deep-research-strategy.md:45:- **KQ-CRIT-4** (iter 4, iter 8): The ai-council "naming drift" is a code-traced, deterministic emitter/validator self-contradiction inside one YAML file, not a registry-vs-YAML drift; it is a precondition for KQ1/KQ6 benchmark validity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/001-gpt-behavioral-hardening-research/research/lineages/sonnet-critical/iterations/iteration-004.md:33:Because `route_proof.mode`/`target_agent` (:133-134) can never equal what `route_fields` (:118) actually produces, `post_dispatch_validate` for ai-council would fail this specific check **regardless of which model or executor runs the council workflow** — GPT, Claude, or anything else. This has a direct, important consequence for KQ1/KQ6: if the planned GPT-vs-Claude benchmark (both lineages' KQ6 answer) runs the ai-council mode as-is, **both** legs would show a route-proof failure on this field, which could be misread as "GPT fails ai-council route-proof" (or even "both models fail," muddying the comparison) when the actual cause is a pre-existing validator bug unrelated to either model's behavior. **This must be fixed before the KQ1/KQ6 benchmark's ai-council leg is run, or that leg's results are uninterpretable.** Neither prior lineage flagged this benchmark-validity risk; both treated the naming-drift fix as a KQ3/KQ8 hygiene item, not as a precondition for KQ1/KQ6 result validity.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/001-gpt-behavioral-hardening-research/research/lineages/sonnet-critical/iterations/iteration-004.md:48:newInfoRatio: 0.65 — novelty: reads the emitter block (:117-118) that neither prior lineage's citation distinguished from the validator block (:132-136), reframes the fix as smaller and more surgical, and connects it forward as a benchmark-validity precondition rather than a standalone hygiene item.
/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-deep-loop/031-deep-loop-gpt-reliability/001-research-and-diagnosis/001-gpt-behavioral-hardening-research/research/lineages/sonnet-critical/research.md:81:- **ai-council benchmark validity**: NEW residual — the KQ1/KQ6 benchmark's ai-council leg is uninterpretable until Deliverable 2 lands.

The delivered work added an append-only audit journal, an improvement-scoped mutation coverage graph, dimension trade-off detection, candidate lineage tracking for optional parallel waves, and benchmark stability scoring so the improver loop could behave like the deeper research and review loops instead of remaining an isolated evaluator.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:56:Before this phase shipped, the improve-agent loop had strong evaluator logic but weak runtime truth. It lacked a dedicated audit journal, had no improvement-scoped coverage graph, could not detect cross-dimension trade-offs, had no candidate-lineage model for parallel exploration, and could not measure benchmark replay stability. That left the loop harder to audit, less explainable, and less aligned with the runtime-truth contracts already established for `sk-deep-research` and `sk-deep-review`.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:95:| `.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` | Created | Improvement-scoped coverage graph and exhausted-mutation tracking. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:150:3. **Given** a reader needs the architectural rationale, **when** they open `decision-record.md`, **then** they can see why journal writes stayed in the orchestrator, why coverage graph reuse was chosen, and how backward compatibility was protected.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:118:| REQ-002 | Research and review must actively consult the coverage graph during live stop evaluation. | Graph upsert and graph convergence calls are documented in the shipped research and review releases. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:142:- **SC-002**: The packet shows that the coverage graph is actively consulted on the live research and review loop paths.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/description.json:3:  \\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Phase 8: Further deep-loop improvements — land 12 P0/P1/P2 recommendations surfaced by the 20-iteration deep-research audit across sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, sk-improve-agent, and the shared coverage graph. 008 does put the coverage graph onto the live research and review YAML paths: `deep_loop_graph_upsert` is present after iteration reduction and `deep_loop_graph_convergence` is present before the inline stop vote in both loop families. The remaining release-level gaps are in contract fidelity around what gets persisted and surfaced: the reducers do not currently expose a trustworthy `graphConvergenceScore`, review-side blocked-stop payloads can serialize blocker objects into a `string[]` contract, session scoping is still optional/incomplete in shared read helpers, and review `next-focus` can stay pinned to an old blocked stop after later iterations.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/scratch/closing-review.md:85:  - REQ-012 and ADR-001/003 treat `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` scoping as mandatory, but the handlers still intentionally fall back to `all_sessions_default` aggregation when `sessionId` is omitted. all listed research relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; research metrics use the defined formulas. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:342:| REQ-004 | Review coverage graphs MUST use the defined node kinds, relation types, initial weight estimates, and metadata contract. | `DIMENSION`, `FILE`, `FINDING`, `EVIDENCE`, and `REMEDIATION` are canonical; all listed review relations appear unchanged; initial weight estimates are documented as inherited starting points and a calibration task is carried through plan/tasks; review metrics use the defined formulas. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:353:| REQ-010 | Human-readable graph status summaries MUST be part of the contract, not a debugging afterthought. | `deep_loop_graph_status` returns documented counts and signal summaries suitable for dashboards and synthesis surfaces; standalone visualization is deferred to a later phase. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:361:- **SC-001**: The phase documents the coverage graph as the fourth graph system in the Spec Kit Memory stack and clearly reuses existing graph patterns.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md:455:- As a maintainer of deep review, I want coverage graph queries that show which files, dimensions, and findings still need evidence so reducer decisions stay explainable.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict.      sessionId,\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5574:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5581:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5584:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5586:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5588:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5596:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5600:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5641:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5647:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5681:Description: `insertEdge()` synthesizes missing nodes without any `sessionId`, but every session-aware reader/filter only trusts `node.sessionId` or `node.metadata.sessionId`. That means a caller can build a graph entirely through the public insert API, attach `metadata.sessionId` to edges, and still lose those nodes from `getNodes()` and any signal helper that depends on node-level session matching. This makes the session-aware export surface easy to misuse and hard to reason about.  \\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5688:sessionId: typeof metadata.sessionId === 'string' && metadata.sessionId ? metadata.sessionId : undefined,\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5691:if (typeof node.sessionId === 'string' && node.sessionId) return node.sessionId;\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5693:if (matchesSession(graph, node, sessionId, 'node')) results.push(node);\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5695:Recommendation: Centralize graph/session normalization in one shared helper and either propagate `metadata.sessionId` into synthesized nodes or add an explicit node upsert API. Add direct session-scoped tests for `getNodes()`, `getEdges()`, `getEdgesFrom()`, `getEdgesTo()`, and the signal helpers.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5703:Description: The CJS helper exports `computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId)` as a recent-edge counter, while the canonical runtime implementation exports `computeMomentum(specFolder, loopType)` as a snapshot-to-snapshot delta calculator in [.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:555). Same name, different meaning. That is a long-term drift trap for maintainers, especially because the requested test suite only validates the CJS behavior in [.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/coverage-graph-signals.vitest.ts:117).  \\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5707:function computeMomentum(graph, nodeId, windowSize, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5748:function reportContradictions(graph, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-004.md:5754:function contradictionDensity(graph, sessionId) {\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:3:description: \\\\\\\"Four-phase implementation delivering runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across 100 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review.\\\\\\\"\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:71:1. **Lane 1 — Claim-Adjudication Stop Gate Wiring** closed F002 and F007 by adding a universal STOP veto pre-check and a dedicated `claimAdjudicationGate` in the review legal-stop decision tree, plus persisted `claim_adjudication` events, typed packet docs in `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md` §9, and matching `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md` §Step 4a. New REQs: REQ-026, REQ-027.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:72:2. **Lane 2 — Coverage-Graph Session Isolation** closed F004, F005, F006, and F013 by bumping `deep-loop-graph.sqlite` to v2 with a composite primary key of `(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)` on both `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges`, migrating via drop-and-recreate, adding a shared-ID collisions regression to `session-isolation.vitest.ts`, and documenting the typed `graphEvents` payload shape and namespace rules in both state_format references. New REQs: REQ-028, REQ-029.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:75:5. **Lane 5 — Release-Readiness Reconciliation** closed F013, F014, F015, and F016 by rewriting the phase-008 closeout surfaces, the packet-root completion surfaces below, and the reducer-owned `ACTIVE RISKS` dashboard section so non-P0 release-readiness debt stays visible. New REQ: REQ-034.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:114:| Closing-audit Lane 1–5 remediation (REQ-026 through REQ-034) | PASS — see `008-further-deep-loop-improvements/implementation-summary.md` §Closing Audit Remediation Notes and `review/review-report.md` |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:42:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"D3: Is the mutation coverage graph namespace (`loop_type: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"`) properly isolated from the deep-research/deep-review namespaces in the shared SQLite store?\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:72:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"D5: Does any loop phase (init / iteration / convergence / synthesis) actively READ from the coverage graph (`coverage-graph-query.ts`, `coverage-graph-convergence.cjs`) to inform decisions, or only WRITE to it?\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:90:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"D5: Does the coverage graph's contribution to the 3-signal convergence math exceed its weight, or is it merely nominal?\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:301:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"`sk-improve-agent` follows a different path entirely: its resume contract replays a local \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"coverage graph + registry\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", and `mutation-coverage.cjs` persists an `improvement` JSON graph with `fs`/`path` helpers and `loopType: 'improvement'`, while the agent only consumes an orchestrator-provided summary as advisory context. That means it does not supply the missing shared `deep_loop_graph_*` bridge for research/review convergence (.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300; .opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:9-10; .opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:21; .opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs:50-67; .opencode/agents/agent-improver.md:175-177).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:319:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Both deep-loop convergence contracts still document a reducer-owned in-memory graph bridge that is not present in the live reducer: deep research says \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" when `graphEvents` exist, and deep review says the same for review iterations (.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205; .opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:658-683).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:711:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The published deep-research contract says that when `graphEvents` exist, \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"the reducer builds an in-memory coverage graph and derives additional signals for the legal-stop gate evaluation,\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" including graph coverage sub-checks (`.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md:1181-1205`). The live research reducer does not implement that bridge: its only imports are `fs` and `path`, and its convergence score is taken from `latestIteration.convergenceSignals?.compositeStop` or `latestIteration?.newInfoRatio` rather than any graph helper (`.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:11-13`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:244-249`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1177:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The resume contract promises richer recovery than the shipped journal helpers provide. The skill says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the journal module only exposes raw-event readback, `getLastIteration()`, and `getSessionResult()`. If an interruption happens before a `session_ended` event is written, `getSessionResult()` returns `{ stopReason: null, sessionOutcome: null }`, so the helper surface itself does not reconstruct candidate, gate, benchmark, or trade-off state (`.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-300`, `.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:332-339`, `.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:148-203`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1263:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The published resume contract promises more reconstruction than the shipped recovery helper exposes. Both the command and skill say resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch, but the only recovery-facing journal helpers return the max iteration number and the terminal `stopReason/sessionOutcome`; there is no helper that rebuilds blocked-stop payloads, trade-off state, benchmark warnings, or lineage context from raw journal events (`.opencode/commands/deep/start-agent-improvement-loop.md:334-339`, `.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:290-295`, `.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs:168-203`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1530:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1551:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1735:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"A different namespace-isolation weakness does exist on the shared read side for research/review sessions: the convergence handler never accepts `sessionId`, constructs its namespace from only `{ specFolder, loopType }`, and then calls `getStats()` plus `computeSignals()` / `computeMomentum()` without session scoping, so convergence decisions aggregate all sessions sharing the same spec folder and loop type (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:47-52`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:92-111`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:511-545`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-signals.ts:163-220`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1779:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The published `sk-improve-agent` contract and visible auto workflow keep that mutation coverage graph local to the improvement runtime rather than bridging it into the shared coverage-graph MCP. The skill describes `scripts/mutation-coverage.cjs` as the reader/writer for the coverage graph and says it uses `loop_type: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"improvement\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` isolation, while the auto workflow only appends the state ledger and runs `scripts/reduce-state.cjs`; I found no visible `deep_loop_graph_upsert` step on this path (`.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:298-300`, `.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:389-390`, `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:149-155`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1801:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The query surface only partially exposes session isolation. `handleCoverageGraphQuery()` accepts `sessionId` and passes it into `ns`, but the inspected helper queries for coverage gaps, contradictions, and unverified claims read by `spec_folder` and `loop_type` only, ignoring `ns.sessionId`; this means research/review sessions can be co-mingled on read even though improvement-mode entries are blocked from the DB entirely (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:28-35`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:55-58`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:63-126`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:136-147`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-query.ts:238-277`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:1823:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The shared SQLite coverage graph does not admit `improvement` loop types at all. The DB layer defines `LoopType = 'research' | 'review'`, constrains valid kinds/relations by those two namespaces, and the schema enforces `CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review'))`; the MCP upsert handler repeats the same validation and rejects any other value (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:17`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:136-146`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-157`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:51-53`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/upsert.ts:72-80`).\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2121:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"`SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2513:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"I did not find a shipped orchestrator implementation file that performs the promised “replay journal + coverage graph + registry before dispatch,” so this pass had to prove absence via reducer inputs and helper interfaces rather than a single end-to-end resume driver.\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/findings-registry.json:2558:      \\\\\\\"text\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"A command-doc requirement to invoke structural graph tools before or during iterations; the inspected command surfaces only bootstrap CocoIndex.\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:130:### Dashboard-only ancestry |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-020.md:7:- `SKILL.md` says resume replays the journal, coverage graph, and registry before dispatch and that the orchestrator skips mutation types already in the exhausted log, but the visible autonomous loop never invokes `mutation-coverage.cjs` before candidate generation. The shipped loop proceeds from integration scan directly to candidate generation, scoring, benchmarking, ledger append, reducer refresh, and reducer-driven stop check, so coverage state does not visibly steer mutation focus selection. (`.opencode/skills/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:294-300`, `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:137-158`)\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:2:  \\\\\\\"sessionId\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:52:      \\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"`coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\\\\\\",\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-findings-registry.json:272:      \\\\\\\"description\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"The research loop protocol still shows `{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"event\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"event\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"resumed\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fromIteration\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":N}` at `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage memory-platform redesign work from other research bundles unless it is directly required by the scoped runtime improvements in this packet.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:7:- The standalone graph helper does have bounded weight, not runaway influence: `computeGraphConvergence()` builds `graphScore` from fragmentation (0.25), normalized depth (0.25), question coverage (0.30), and source diversity (0.20), then limits the merged result to `blendedScore = compositeStop * 0.6 + graphScore * 0.4`. Inside this helper, the coverage graph cannot contribute more than 40% of the final blended scalar (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/coverage-graph-convergence.cjs:141-205`).\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/iterations/iteration-014.md:10:- The shipped deep-review reducer mirrors the same pattern. `computeConvergenceScore()` reads `latest.convergenceSignals.compositeStop` or falls back to `1 - newFindingsRatio`, and the reducer then publishes only that scalar as `convergenceScore`. No inspected review reducer code reconstructs a coverage graph or invokes graph convergence logic before writing the dashboard/registry (`.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:360-390`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:579-583`, `.opencode/skills/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:671-680`).\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:sessionId: rvw-2026-04-12T11-30-00Z\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:32:This bundle delivered runtime truth, semantic coverage graph, wave execution, and offline optimization across ~200 files and +19K lines for sk-deep-research, sk-deep-review, and sk-improve-agent.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:59:Prior session context: A previous 10-iteration review (rvw-2026-04-11T13-50-06Z) found 16 findings (0 P0 / 10 P1 / 6 P2) with CONDITIONAL verdict. REQ-004 | Segment-local JSONL and merge rules MUST preserve auditability when wave results are merged back into the main packet lineage. | Segment artifacts remain replayable on their own, merged records always include explicit keys `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, and merge logic never relies on append order as the source of truth. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6269:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. That means two different segment-local findings that both use `F-001` are treated as the same logical record even when their evidence differs. The same defect appears in segment-state merge logic (`wave-segment-state.cjs:199-247`), so cross-segment collisions can silently flatten lineage during board updates and replay merges.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-005.md:6320:- Description: Phase 003 requires merge lineage to be keyed by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, but `mergeFinding()` looks up existing records with `f.findingId === findingRecord.findingId`. truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 into the sk-improve-agent skill across four sub-phases. Sub-phase 5a delivers the core runtime contracts (stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journal, hypothesis ledger). Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. All new config fields are optional with defaults, preserving backward compatibility.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:650:- [ ] 042 Phase 2 coverage graph API confirmed to support `loop_type` namespace parameter\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:708:Add mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, and exhausted-mutations logging. When Opus receives a complex prompt with `--effort high`, it generates an internal chain-of-thought before responding. This matters for architecture trade-off analysis, root cause debugging, and multi-dimensional decision-making where surface-level pattern matching falls short. The reasoning is not just longer responses. It is a fundamentally different processing mode that considers alternatives, backtracks, and synthesizes before committing to an answer.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2410:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:**Context**: Phase 2 built a coverage graph in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agents/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agents/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. Open question: how should unresolved nodes from retired segments contribute to the final global stop-decision trace?\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4720:- Normalize merge on explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, `run`, `findingId`. Never trust append order.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:19:- **F004**: Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide — `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154` — `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:27:| spec_code | fail | hard | `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99` | REQ-012 requires shared graph helpers to scope by `specFolder + loopType + sessionId`, but the underlying write identity is still global by bare `id`. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The `all_sessions_default` path is explicitly documented bootstrap/debug behavior rather than a hidden bypass — `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:70`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:174`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:3:description: \\\\\\\"Deliver the deep-loop coverage graph substrate by extracting shared graph primitives, adding dedicated storage and MCP tools, and wiring graph-backed convergence into deep research and deep review.\\\\\\\"\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:7:  - sk-deep-review.\\\\\\\"\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1374:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/implementation-summary.md:60:Phases were delivered in strict dependency order: runtime truth first, then coverage graph, then wave execution, then offline optimization. The original four phases were independently validated against their task lists and quality gates.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1379:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:55:- v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation using the Phase 002 coverage graph once the graph surfaces are operational.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1380:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:87:- Add v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation that refines decomposition using the Phase 002 coverage graph once it is operational.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1384:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:153:| REQ-005 | Each segment MUST use Phase 002 graph signals independently for convergence, pruning, promotion, and v2 decomposition upgrades. | Segment convergence explicitly references coverage-graph status or convergence outputs; prune logic can retire converged or stuck segments without dropping unresolved global gaps silently; graph-enhanced segmentation is blocked until the Phase 002 coverage graph is operational. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1393:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/plan.md:264:| Phase 3b graph-enhanced segmentation | Medium | Depends on Phase 002 coverage graph being operational and stable enough to refine v1 safely. |\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1405:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:19:- **F004**: Coverage-graph writes are not session-isolated when IDs collide — `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:154` — `coverage_nodes` and `coverage_edges` key rows by bare `id` only (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:152-192`), and both upsert paths decide update vs insert with `WHERE id = ?` instead of the full namespace (`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:292-302`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/coverage-graph/coverage-graph-db.ts:370-376`). A later session that reuses an earlier node or edge ID mutates the existing row rather than creating a session-local record, violating REQ-012’s `specFolder + loopType + sessionId` isolation contract (`.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/008-further-deep-loop-improvements/spec.md:99-100`) and creating a cross-session overwrite/leak path underneath handlers that otherwise pass `sessionId`.\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1408:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-002.md:41:- Handler fallback without `sessionId`: The `all_sessions_default` path is explicitly documented bootstrap/debug behavior rather than a hidden bypass — `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/query.ts:70`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/convergence.ts:174`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/coverage-graph/status.ts:71`\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1409:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:37:6. Convergence per segment: Each segment needs its own convergence assessment. Should each segment have its own coverage graph instance? Or one graph with segment-scoped queries?\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1424:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4704:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. Findings: convergence docs already say segment filtering should apply only to the current segment while hard stops still count all iterations and cross-segment analysis still reads the full JSONL ([convergence.md:23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L23), [convergence.md:305](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/references/convergence.md#L305)); the workers already expect the orchestrator to add `segment` and `convergenceSignals` to iteration records ([deep-research.md:189](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agents/deep-research.md#L189), [deep-review.md:253](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/agents/deep-review.md#L253)); review STOP is explicitly global because it depends on total P0/P1 counts, full-dimension coverage, and global quality gates ([spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:340](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/commands/speckit/assets/speckit_deep-review_auto.yaml#L340)). Novel insight: one global coverage graph plus segment-scoped queries is cleaner than spinning up a separate graph per segment. Open question: how should unresolved nodes from retired segments contribute to the final global stop-decision trace?\\\\n.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-008.md:1425:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:4752:6. **Convergence per segment**. Focus: whether each segment needs its own graph/coverage instance. 60 seconds. When you save, update or delete a memory, the cache for affected searches clears automatically. You never see stale results.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:543:**Read-path readiness:** `ensureCodeGraphReady()` runs automatically inside `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context`. It checks graph freshness, returns a `readiness` block, and performs bounded inline selective reindex only when the stale set is small enough to repair safely on the read path. Empty graphs, large stale sets, and other full-scan cases remain explicit `code_graph_scan` work.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:601:Resume session with combined memory, code graph and CocoIndex status in a single call. Use when you want the detailed merged resume payload directly. The response carries freshness-aware code-graph status (`fresh`, `stale`, `empty`, `error`) instead of count-only health. For the canonical first-call recovery path on session start or after `/clear`, prefer `session_bootstrap`.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:765:Run a health check. This is the diagnostic tool for when search quality degrades or something feels off. It checks for stale indexes, divergent aliases, broken embeddings and other issues. It can also attempt automatic repairs.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:779:Check session readiness: priming status, code graph freshness and time since last tool call. Returns `ok`, `warning` or `stale` with actionable hints. Call periodically during long sessions to detect context drift.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1062:Query structural code relationships: `outline` (file symbols), `calls_from` and `calls_to` (call graph), `imports_from` and `imports_to` (dependency graph). Use this instead of Grep for structural queries. Supports multi-hop BFS traversal. [trackedFile],\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/crash-recovery.vitest.ts:979:  it('cleanupOrphans removes stale nodes/edges', () => {\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:342:After every successful save, a consolidation cycle hook fires when `SPECKIT_CONSOLIDATION` is enabled (default ON). The N3-lite consolidation engine scans for contradictions (memory pairs above 0.85 cosine similarity with negation keyword conflicts), runs Hebbian strengthening on recently accessed edges (+0.05 per cycle with a 30-day decay), detects stale edges (unfetched for 90+ days) and enforces edge bounds (maximum 20 per node). The cycle runs on a weekly cadence.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:348:Successful insertions now clear the search cache immediately instead of waiting for delete-time invalidation or TTL expiry. `index_memory()` calls `clear_search_cache()` after the transactional insert, active-projection update and optional `vec_memories` write succeed, so a brand-new memory becomes visible to repeated `memory_search` calls right away. The fix closes a stale-results gap where the save path could report success while cached searches still replayed a pre-insert snapshot.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:370:By default, if embedding regeneration fails (API timeout, provider outage), the entire update rolls back with no changes applied. Nothing happens. With `allowPartialUpdate` enabled, the metadata changes persist and the embedding is marked as pending for later re-indexing by the next `memory_index_scan`. That mode is useful when you need to fix metadata urgently and can tolerate a temporarily stale embedding.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:769:Recovery is automatic and requires no user intervention. If the pending file is stale (the DB row was never committed), it is logged and left for manual review rather than silently deleted.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:787:The archival sweep runs periodically and respects tier-based protection: constitutional and critical-tier memories are never auto-archived. Access tracker data (`access_count`, `last_accessed`) drives the dormancy decision. On unarchive, BM25 is restored from stored text fields, while vector re-embedding is explicitly deferred and logged for the next index scan (no immediate vector row recreation). The archival manager lazy-loads the tier classifier to avoid circular dependencies at import time.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:809:Edge bounds are enforced at insert time. Auto-generated edges (those with `created_by='auto'`) are rejected when a node already has 20 edges (`MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE`) and clamped to a maximum strength of 0.5 (`MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH`). Every strength modification is logged to a `weight_history` table recording old strength, new strength, who changed it, when and why. The `created_by` and `last_accessed` fields on each edge track provenance and usage patterns.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1561:Every causal edge now carries `created_by` and `last_accessed` metadata fields tracking who created the edge and when it was last used. All strength modifications are logged to a `weight_history` table recording old strength, new strength, the actor (`changed_by`), timestamp and reason.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:1565:This audit infrastructure supports the N3-lite consolidation engine: Hebbian strengthening, staleness detection and edge bounds enforcement all rely on accurate weight history and provenance tracking.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2015:**C2: Citation fallback chain:** `composite-scoring.ts` fell back through `last_accessed` then `updated_at` when no citation data existed, conflating recency with citation authority. The fallback chain was removed. The function returns 0 when no citation data exists.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2095:When you ask the same question twice within a short time, the system should not redo all the expensive work. This feature remembers recent results for up to 60 seconds so repeat requests get instant answers from the cache. When you save, update or delete a memory, the cache for affected searches is cleared automatically so you never see stale results.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2117:The access tracker (`lib/storage/access-tracker.ts`) implements batched access counting with a soft-accumulator pattern. Each retrieval hit increments an in-memory accumulator by 0.1. When the accumulator exceeds the 0.5 threshold, a database write flushes the accumulated count to the `access_count` column in `memory_index` and updates `last_accessed`. This batching reduces write amplification from high-frequency search operations.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2514:is missing or stale (spec.md modified after description.json).\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:2538:discovery, fresh per-folder files win, stale or corrupt existing files are\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3135:The legacy V1 pipeline was the root cause of 3 of 4 P0 bugs: an inverted `STATE_PRIORITY` map, divergent scoring order in post-search weighting and a mismatched deep-query variant cap. Since V2 was already the default, the legacy handler path in `memory-search.ts` was removed and the 4-stage orchestrator became the only runtime path. Stage helpers with familiar names now live in stage modules (`stage1-candidate-gen.ts`, `stage2-fusion.ts`, `stage3-rerank.ts`, `stage4-filter.ts`) rather than the old monolithic V1 branch. The `isPipelineV2Enabled()` function now always returns `true` with a deprecation comment, and stale legacy-handler imports were removed.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3218:**IMPLEMENTED (Sprint 019).** Startup in `context-server.ts` uses `server.setInstructions()` to inject a dynamic memory-system overview (total memories, spec folder count, channels, stale count) into the MCP instruction payload. Reuses existing `memory_stats` logic. Gated by `SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_INIT` (default `true`).\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3262:When another process changes the database while the server is running, the server needs to notice and reconnect. This feature watches for a signal file that says \\\\\\\"the database changed\\\\\\\" and automatically refreshes the connection. Without it, the server would keep using stale data until someone manually restarted it.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3432:Over time, stored memories can contradict each other or grow stale. This feature runs periodic housekeeping to spot conflicts, strengthen connections that get used often and flag relationships that have not been touched in months. Think of it as a librarian who regularly walks the shelves to catch duplicate entries and retire outdated references.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:3843:When you delete or rename a file on your computer, the search index needs to clean up the old entry so it does not show stale results. This feature handles that cleanup automatically. Without it, you could search and find references to files that no longer exist, like a phone book that still lists people who have moved away.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:4425:| `SPECKIT_CONSOLIDATION` | `true` | boolean | `lib/search/search-flags.ts` | Enables the N3-lite consolidation engine which runs after every successful save. Instructions are computed once at startup and not refreshed during session (CHK-076). All strength modifications are logged to a `weight_history` table recording old strength, new strength, the actor (`changed_by`), timestamp and reason.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/10--graph-signal-activation/04-weight-history-audit-tracking.md:22:This audit infrastructure supports the N3-lite consolidation engine: Hebbian strengthening, staleness detection and edge bounds enforcement all rely on accurate weight history and provenance tracking.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-folder-detector-functional.js:295:    `).run('099-stale-folder', 'T-stale', 'preflight');\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/18-session-resume-tool.md:12:The session_resume handler performs three sub-calls: (1) `memory_context` with `mode=resume` and `profile=resume` to recover session state, (2) code graph database query for node/edge/file counts and last scan timestamp, and (3) CocoIndex binary availability check via filesystem probe. It also appends the shared structural ready/stale/missing contract from `session-snapshot.ts`, so callers can tell when a deeper refresh is needed. Results are merged into a `SessionResumeResult` with `memory`, `codeGraph`, `cocoIndex`, optional `structuralContext`, and `hints` fields. Failures in any sub-call are captured as error entries with recovery hints rather than failing the entire call. For the canonical first-call recovery step, use `session_bootstrap`; `session_resume` remains the detailed merged surface.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/17-session-health-tool.md:3:description: \\\\\\\"MCP tool (session_health) that reports session readiness with ok/warning/stale status, code graph freshness, priming status, and quality score.\\\\\\\"\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/17-session-health-tool.md:10:MCP tool (session_health) that reports session readiness with ok/warning/stale status, code graph freshness, priming status, and quality score.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/17-session-health-tool.md:12:The session_health handler computes session status based on three time thresholds: ok (last tool call within 15 minutes), warning (within 60 minutes), stale (beyond 60 minutes or session older than 24 hours). It returns structured details including sessionAgeMs, lastToolCallAgoMs, graphFreshness (fresh/stale/empty/error), specFolder, primingStatus, and a QualityScore with 4 weighted factors (recency, recovery, graphFreshness, continuity). Actionable hints guide the user toward recovery actions. The session_health tool call is excluded from recordToolCall() to prevent idle timer reset — health checks no longer artificially extend session freshness.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:11:    expect(normalizeStructuralReadiness('stale')).toBe('stale');\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:18:      graphFreshness: 'stale',\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-ops-hardening.vitest.ts:22:    expect(contract.readiness.canonical).toBe('stale');\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/full-spec-doc-indexing.vitest.ts:869:        last_accessed: 0,\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/full-spec-doc-indexing.vitest.ts:912:        last_accessed: 0,\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/15-code-graph-auto-trigger.md:12:The ensure-ready module checks graph state (fresh/stale/empty) by comparing the current git HEAD against the last indexed HEAD and scanning for stale files. handlers.\\\\n/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/15-code-graph-auto-trigger.md:28:|
